etymology.description: 人(人) (rén, 'person') with headdress -> stylized as 夭 (young man) -> 攴(攴) 'strike' added -> man stylized as 方 (fāng, 'square') -> 敖 (áo, 'proud; arrogant')
etymology.interpretation: The original depiction of a person with a headdress symbolized authority or pride, hence 'proud; arrogant'. Over time, the person was stylized as a youthful figure, indicating vigor. The addition of 攴(攴) (pū, 'strike') may suggest authority or control, reinforcing the proud or domineering sense. The stylization into 方 (fāng) further abstracted the form to preserve its phonetic function.

examples.chinese: 他在比赛中表现得非常骄傲和自信(他在比賽中表現得非常驕傲和自信)。
examples.pinyin: Tā zài bǐsài zhōng biǎoxiàn de fēicháng jiāo'ào hé zìxìn.
examples.english: He behaved very proudly and confidently in the competition.

examples.chinese: 她的成功让她变得更加自豪和骄傲(她的成功讓她變得更加自豪和驕傲)。
examples.pinyin: Tā de chénggōng ràng tā biàn de gèng jiā zìháo hé jiāo'ào.
examples.english: Her success made her even more proud and arrogant.

examples.chinese: 骄傲的人常常忽视他人的意见(驕傲的人常常忽視他人的意見)。
examples.pinyin: Jiāo'ào de rén chángcháng hūshì tārén de yìjiàn.
examples.english: Proud people often ignore the opinions of others.
